content.text: The burn happens once. Everything after is pulling a trigger.

Casting is two acts. Weaving is the expensive one — the roll, the heat, the risk — and it banks multiple uses of a pattern. Firing spends one banked use as a cheap, clean action. One cast goes a long way.

Banked uses are called rounds, because patterns load like ammunition and the gun language is not a metaphor: patterns are licensed corporate IP, rounds are inventoried, and unlicensed weaving is the crime. The patterns themselves — stats, licenses, bootlegs — live on Patterns. This page is the grammar that loads and fires them.

The grammar adds no new rolls and is the same for anyone who can weave: the Weave is a Check, the Fire is an Attack (both per The Two Rolls). Weaving is unlocked through the Channeler tree's entry augment, Rift Conduit — see the Channeler tree — but nothing on this page changes once you have it.
Weave — 3 AP in combat, or a downtime / rest-node action
Pick a pattern you know and make the Check: Rift Lore accuracy dice + Interface vs the pattern's Weave DC (pattern stats — see Patterns).

Full: bank the pattern's listed rounds. Partial: bank them anyway and take +1 extra Charge — it fought you. Fail: bank nothing and take +1 Charge; if your Charge sits at or past the misfire threshold (see Charge & Saturation), roll on the Misfire Table below.
Take Charge equal to the pattern's tier — every Weave, full or failed. Weaving in combat is hot work: +1 Charge on top.
the rift doesn't do refunds.
Round capacity: 4 + Interface bonus (bonus table — see Stats & Growth). Rounds persist until fired — weave in downtime or at rest nodes and arrive at the mission loaded.
Fire — the pattern's AP cost, spend 1 round
Firing carries no heat and no risk — the round is stable. The danger lives where the heat lives: at the Weave.
Targeted patterns are Attacks: your focus's accuracy die + Interface vs Evasion. Crits pay the pattern's beats.
Cluster patterns roll nothing. They hit the zone.
Self, ally, and touch patterns are not Attacks — pay the AP, spend the round, the effect happens.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: Targeted patterns fire on "your focus's accuracy die," but no rebuild doc defines focus hardware stats — accuracy die, price, or weight. A targeted pattern cannot resolve at the table until the focus gets a gear entry.
Misfire
A Weave that fails while your Charge is at or past the misfire threshold (see Charge & Saturation) misfires. Bootleg patterns open that window earlier (see Patterns). Roll d6:

rows: Backlash — 1d6 direct damage to the weaver, +1 Charge. The pattern bites the hand.
Wild discharge — the pattern fires now at half effect, centered on a cluster of the GM's choice — including yours.
Corrupt rounds — the banked rounds take a quirk: wrong color, wrong sound, one effect line warped (the GM's line). They still fire. Probably.
Surge — +2 Charge, the pattern is lost, and everything chrome in your Adjacent cluster flickers. The corps log these.

content.text: everyone's got a misfire story. the ones who don't are the story.
Weave events at this conduit are logged and reported to the licensing authority as a condition of registration. Surge signatures are non-deletable. Thank you for weaving responsibly.

content.label: AUGMENT HOOKS
content.text: Channeler-tree augments extend this grammar — deeper capacity, hot-loaded rounds, lending rounds to allies, better venting — without changing anything printed here. See the Channeler tree. Charge, venting, and Saturation live on Charge & Saturation.
